RaveSpin
Features Demo Tech Deep dive About

About

Project context, author, and how to get the code.

What is RaveSpin?

RaveSpin is a tool that lets DJs use XR (VR/AR) representations of their gear so they can mix and perform on the go—without carrying decks or a laptop. You put on a compatible headset (e.g. Meta Quest 2, 3, or Pro), see a virtual DDJ-FLX4-style controller, load tracks from an in-headset library, and mix with two decks, EQ, crossfader, and beat FX. It’s built for practice, experimentation, and showing what’s possible when DJing meets XR.

Who made it?

César Hannin (student ID: C21415142). RaveSpin is the Fourth Year Final Project for 2025–2026 at TU Dublin, Grangegorman.

Repository & license

Source code: github.com/marc-rene/RaveSpin. The main project does not include a root LICENSE file; add-on code (e.g. godot-xr-tools, waveform_generator, GD Audio Analyzer) may have their own licenses (e.g. MIT). Check each add-on folder if you plan to reuse or distribute code.

Demo video

Watch RaveSpin in action: YouTube demo.

Documentation in the repo

Inside the RaveSpin repo you’ll find:

  • Documentation/Assets/ — Draw.io diagrams: System Overview, User Interface Draft, and use cases (e.g. “Using Ravespin,” “User invites another user,” “2 Users perform together using Rave-Spin multiplayer”).
  • README.md — Short project description and demo link.
  • Godot/ — Full Godot project (scenes, scripts, assets, addons).

No GitHub Actions workflow is committed for building or deploying the app; export is done from Godot. This website lives under Documentation/Website and can be built with Node or Bun for deployment to GitHub Pages (see the site’s README).
